message:
feat(mvcc): atomic amortizers - Cicada read-ts + Hekaton TID gap (IMPL-14/15)

Adds `atomic_amortizers` module with two amortization primitives that
trade a single fetch_add for N-way local issuance:

* ReadTsBatcher (IMPL-14 / Cicada): per-thread batch reservation via
  thread_local. `next_read_ts()` hands values from a local range, only
  issuing a shared fetch_add when the batch is exhausted. Amortizes N
  read-ts reservations to ~N/batch_size atomics.

* TidGapAllocator (IMPL-15 / Hekaton): single fetch_add reserves a
  contiguous `gap_size` TID range as a TidGap handle; the gap owner
  draws TIDs without any further synchronization. Two concurrent
  reservations are guaranteed non-overlapping by CAS semantics.

Both use saturating_add on the u64 counters so the allocators are
total even at the upper edge of the TID/ts domain. `batch_size` /
`gap_size` of 0 is clamped to 1.

7 tests, all passing:
- Cicada single-thread: 1000 unique monotonic values.
- Cicada multi-thread: 4x250 -> 1000 unique aggregate, monotonic per thread.
- Cicada zero-batch clamp.
- Hekaton: 10 gaps pairwise non-overlapping, per-gap exhaustion semantics.
- Hekaton multi-thread: 4x25 -> 100 non-overlapping, unique start values.
- Hekaton gap exhaustion idempotent None.
- Hekaton zero-gap clamp.

No unsafe. No new deps.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
